phasen.label: kennenlernen
phasen.kurzbeschreibung: After ten at night the phone doesn't get put down anymore. Both write about the same, about as fast, about as warm. If anyone had asked in those weeks how it was going, the answer would have been: good.
phasen.dominantes_muster: Message bursts between 9pm and midnight, initiated from both sides.

phasen.label: plateau
phasen.kurzbeschreibung: On the surface everything keeps going. Under it, something has tipped, but so quietly both could ignore it. Person A writes more often than needed, Person B answers later than before. No fights — just a rhythm that isn't synced anymore.
phasen.dominantes_muster: Multiple messages from Person A, reply from Person B after 3–18 hours.

phasen.label: distanzierung
phasen.titel: When one side writes against the silence
phasen.kurzbeschreibung: Person A is carrying the relationship alone. Emotional monologues, "you still there?" questions, sweet notes as insurance. Person B replies shorter, less often, deflects. The chat reads like two people no longer in the same room.
phasen.dominantes_muster: 70/30 asymmetry, Person B's reply times in the 24h+ range.

phasen.label: konflikt
phasen.titel: The night it gets said out loud
phasen.kurzbeschreibung: Person A breaks the unwritten rule of not talking about the state of things. "I can't keep doing this" — direct, no hedging. Person B stays silent for 52 hours and then answers with a sentence that decides nothing. In that moment the relationship is no longer what it was.
phasen.dominantes_muster: Long direct question, heavily delayed evasive answer.

kipppunkte.beschreibung: At 10:30 pm Person A sends a long, honest text. Three messages in a row, unfiltered. Person B takes 36 hours and replies with a line that pushes it all aside. That's the moment Person A does the quiet math: if I open up, nothing comes back. From here on Person A writes less hopefully, more stubbornly.
For the first time there's no hedging around it. The question is now in the room, and no answer will make it un-asked. Silence is an answer now too.

gesamtbogen: Four months from mutual warmth to one-sided relationship work. Not through a fight, not through cheating — through rhythms that slowly drifted apart while both pretended everything was fine. In the end Person A says out loud what had been felt for months, and Person B answers — as always — too late and too little.
